compiled_state.title: COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md: record-test-status.sh runs before git add -> diff_hash mismatch blocks commit
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3.5 instructs running record-test-status.sh before Step 4 (git add -u). However, the pre-commit test gate validates against the *staged* diff hash. Recording before staging captures the unstaged diff hash, which differs from the staged hash after git add — causing the pre-commit hook to reject the commit with 'code changed since tests were recorded'.

## Reproduction
Follow COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md in order (Step 3.5 record → Step 4 stage → Step 6 commit):
- record-test-status.sh records diff_hash=<unstaged>
- git add -u changes the staged index
- pre-commit hook computes diff_hash=<staged> → mismatch → blocked

## Workaround
Re-run record-test-status.sh after git add (before commit). This contradicts the documented step order.

## Fix
Either:
(a) Move Step 3.5 (record-test-status.sh) to after Step 4 (git add) in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md, or
(b) Make record-test-status.sh use the staged index (git diff --cached) instead of the working tree diff when files are staged.

comments.body: Investigation complete: bug is invalid. COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md already has correct ordering — record-test-status.sh at Step 4.5 (after git add at Step 4). Line 366 explicitly documents why. Additionally, compute-diff-hash.sh uses git diff HEAD which is staging-invariant.
comments.author: Test
